text:
*[Bug 19036] model.matrix(fit, newdata) doesn't respect factors levels from glm fit but does for lm*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19036>

Mauricio Vargas (<mailto:mvargassepulveda@gmail.com|mvargassepulveda@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |mvargassepulveda@gmail.com

--- Comment #2 from Mauricio Vargas (<mailto:mvargassepulveda@gmail.com|mvargassepulveda@gmail.com>) ---
I found the same on my end.

I propose this correction as I added to my source copy here:
<https://github.com/pachadotdev/r-source/commit/ba6ea5a1f76ec4f61a37a26ca102826ef0871e3d#diff-1345dbc1e43a5e2594f6d44f707d96e7fd539bccb4d081bdf7fcac535ae54794>

In this section of `src/library/stats/R/glm.R`


```
...
nargs <- dots[match(c("data", "na.action", "subset"), names(dots), 0L)]
    if (length(nargs) || is.null(formula$model)) {
        fcall <- formula$call
        fcall$method <- "model.frame"





        ## need stats:: for non-standard evaluation
        fcall[[1L]] <- quote(stats::glm)



        fcall[names(nargs)] <- nargs
        env <- environment(formula$terms) %||% parent.frame()
        eval(fcall, env)
    }
    else formula$model
}
```

I changed `model.frame.glm()` to mirror `model.frame.lm()`:


```
...
nargs <- dots[match(c("data", "na.action", "subset"), names(dots), 0L)]
    if (length(nargs) || is.null(formula$model)) {
        ## mimic glm(method = "model.frame") but call model.frame directly
        ## to support xlev argument (PR#18800)
        fcall <- formula$call
        m <- match(c("formula", "data", "subset", "weights", "na.action",
                     "etastart", "mustart", "offset"), names(fcall), 0L)
        fcall <- fcall[c(1L, m)]
        fcall$drop.unused.levels <- TRUE
        ## need stats:: for non-standard evaluation
        fcall[[1L]] <- quote(stats::model.frame)
        fcall$xlev <- formula$xlevels
        ## We want to copy over attributes here, especially predvars.
        fcall$formula <- terms(formula)
        fcall[names(nargs)] <- nargs
        env <- environment(formula$terms) %||% parent.frame()
        eval(fcall, env)
    }
    else formula$model
}
```

elements.text: [Bug 19036] model.matrix(fit, newdata) doesn't respect factors levels from glm fit but does for lm

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |mvargassepulveda@gmail.com

--- Comment #2 from Mauricio Vargas (

...
nargs <- dots[match(c("data", "na.action", "subset"), names(dots), 0L)]
    if (length(nargs) || is.null(formula$model)) {
        fcall <- formula$call
        fcall$method <- "model.frame"





        ## need stats:: for non-standard evaluation
        fcall[[1L]] <- quote(stats::glm)



        fcall[names(nargs)] <- nargs
        env <- environment(formula$terms) %||% parent.frame()
        eval(fcall, env)
    }
    else formula$model
}

...
nargs <- dots[match(c("data", "na.action", "subset"), names(dots), 0L)]
    if (length(nargs) || is.null(formula$model)) {
        ## mimic glm(method = "model.frame") but call model.frame directly
        ## to support xlev argument (PR#18800)
        fcall <- formula$call
        m <- match(c("formula", "data", "subset", "weights", "na.action",
                     "etastart", "mustart", "offset"), names(fcall), 0L)
        fcall <- fcall[c(1L, m)]
        fcall$drop.unused.levels <- TRUE
        ## need stats:: for non-standard evaluation
        fcall[[1L]] <- quote(stats::model.frame)
        fcall$xlev <- formula$xlevels
        ## We want to copy over attributes here, especially predvars.
        fcall$formula <- terms(formula)
        fcall[names(nargs)] <- nargs
        env <- environment(formula$terms) %||% parent.frame()
        eval(fcall, env)
    }
    else formula$model
}

text:
*[Bug 18361] ls.str() not agile to LANGUAGE*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18361>

--- Comment #4 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
Now that `get()` signals classed errors for missing arguments, `print.ls_str`
was fixed to check the class of the error condition in r87080; the fix has been
released in 4.5.0. There is no special handling for variables that legitimately
became missing between the call to `ls.str()` and the `print()` method.

text:
*[Bug 17886] glm.fit says convergence is OK while the likelihood has no maximum*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17886>

Mauricio Vargas (<mailto:mvargassepulveda@gmail.com|mvargassepulveda@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |mvargassepulveda@gmail.com

--- Comment #3 from Mauricio Vargas (<mailto:mvargassepulveda@gmail.com|mvargassepulveda@gmail.com>) ---
(In reply to André Gillibert from comment #2)
Sorry to comment on an old thread, I recently had a meeting with Joao Santos
Silva and I showed him a deviance rule like this,

One of the results he showed me is relevant here, and it is that when the
dependent variable is not a count, some measures of goodness-of-fit for Poisson
regression (like deviance) depend on the scale of the data, and therefore are
uninformative.

There are more details about that here: A cautionary note on goodness-of-fit
statistics for models estimated by pseudo maximum likelihood
<https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/pdf/10.1177/1536867X251341411>

<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19052>

            Bug ID: 19052
           Summary: intToUtf8(-1L) triggers ASAN error
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Linux
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5

Candidate patch:

```
Index: src/main/raw.c
===================================================================
--- src/main/raw.c      (revision 89873)
+++ src/main/raw.c      (working copy)
@@ -393,6 +393,7 @@
        for (i = 0; i < nc; i++) {
            int this = INTEGER(x)[i];
            if (this == NA_INTEGER
+               || this < 0
                || (this >= 0xD800 && this <= 0xDFFF)
                || this > 0x10FFFF)
                SET_STRING_ELT(ans, i, NA_STRING);
@@ -410,6 +411,7 @@
            int this = INTEGER(x)[i];
            if (this == NA_INTEGER
+               || this < 0
                || (this >= 0xDC00 && this <= 0xDFFF)
                || this > 0x10FFFF) {
                haveNA = true;
```

*[Bug 18693] rmultinom()  using LDOUBLE is platform dependent*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18693>

--- Comment #9 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
(In reply to Mauricio Vargas from comment #8)
..................
> I have no idea how to put code side by side here, so I added some extra
> lines to be able to open two windows side by side and see the difference

Please **do** attach a patch file -- bugzilla will allow everyone to see the
two versions of the underlying files, side by side (with color coding;  github
is just *copying* here a feature that existed many decennia before, i.e.
"colorized diff")
.. and usefully call your patch file something like  `rmultinom_PR-18963.diff`
(rather than quite a few other bugzilla helpers who use non-sense file names
such as `patch.diff`).

*[Bug 19053] New: undefined behavior when emitting EOF in file.choose()*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19053>

            Bug ID: 19053
           Summary: undefined behavior when emitting EOF in file.choose()
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Linux
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3675
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3675&action=edit>

  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3677&action=edit>
Patch by Mauricio Vargas

Dear Mauricio Vargas,

Thank you for spending the effort to implement Kahan summation for `rmultinom`!
Here's how to convert your Git commit to a Subversion patch that would work on
Bugzilla:

1. [Check out the Subversion repository] and make that your current directory
2. `wget -O-
<https://github.com/pachadotdev/r-source/commit/a390ce706e6b14a878739ff28a5850a3809d015f.diff>
| patch -p1`
3. `svn diff src/nmath/rmultinom.c > PR18693.patch`

[Check out the Subversion repository]:
<https://contributor.r-project.org/rdevguide/chapters/lifecycle_of_a_patch.html#what-tools-are-required-to-submit-a-patch>

Actually, I've just checked that if I download the `.diff` file from the URL
above, `svn patch $file.diff` works well with it. Since Subversion understands
patch files produced by `git diff`, can it be acceptable to post Git diffs on
Bugzilla?
